---
title: "What PCA actually does: yield curve worked examples"
subtitle: "A short series on Principal Component Analysis applied to yield curves"
author: "Kelvin Wong"
date: "2026-06-01"
categories: [PCA, yield-curves, stress-scenarios, risk-management]
image: thumbnail.jpg
description: "A two-article worked-example series on PCA for yield curves. Article 0 introduces the technique from first principles. Article 1 makes the rotation mechanism visualisable on two tenors. Article 2 extends to three tenors and demonstrates the Level / Slope / Curvature decomposition."
listing:
  contents:
    - "*/index.qmd"
  sort: "order asc"
  type: default
  fields: [order, title, subtitle, date, reading-time]
  field-display-names:
    order: "#"
  categories: false
  sort-ui: false
  filter-ui: false
  id: series-articles
page-layout: full
title-block-banner: false
format:
  html:
    toc: false
---

This series is a worked exposition of Principal Component Analysis applied to yield curve data. It began as personal notes from two encounters with PCA --- once at work, looking at a Dynamic Nelson-Siegel model and noticing the connection to PCA's empirical factors, and again while preparing for the SOA exam. The mechanism only really clicked for me when I drew a 2-tenor rotation plot and could finally *see* what the algorithm was doing to the data.

The series is structured around that visualisation. [Article 0](../pca-yc/article00/index.qmd) introduces the technique from first principles: the stress-testing problem it solves, the mathematics that produces the eigenvalue equation, and the reason maximising variance is the right thing to want. [Article 1](../pca-yc/article01/index.qmd) makes the rotation mechanism visualisable on two tenors. [Article 2](../pca-yc/article02/index.qmd) extends to three tenors and demonstrates the canonical Level / Slope / Curvature decomposition. PCA on real Taiwan TWD data is the subject of [Article 3 of the DNS series](../ics-dns-twd-yc/03-pca-twd/index.qmd) (forthcoming) rather than this one.

::: {#series-articles}
:::

---

Comments and corrections welcome. Please reach out to the author on [GitHub](https://github.com/kelvin1189/actuarial-modeling-notes/issues/new).
